mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts (HOUST) and real sales (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer demand that drives corporate revenue, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals for HOUST and RSAFS fail to produce 60d SPX returns above 1.5% in > 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening manufacturing activity that reduces corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO declines by >1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation (CPIAUCSL above 3%) erodes real interest rates and increases equity risk premiums, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) above 200k signals strong labor market dynamics that boost consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 40% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 200k.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates robust demand-side momentum that drives equity valuations higher, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals for GDPC1 and HOUST fail to produce 20d SPX returns above 1.0% in > 30% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE) signals a supply-demand imbalance that depresses corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls > 1.5% and UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO declines and UNRATE rises simultaneously.
